source_jobs.title: Digital & Advertising Sponsorship Sales Executive
source_jobs.description:
The Company

When it comes to digital communities, The Alliance https://allianceled.io/ is leading the way. Product marketing, sales enablement, product-led growth, AI, customer success, CMOs, you name it, we’re home to thriving communities for them all.

We connect industry leaders and innovators through unparalleled networking opportunities. We bring together leading minds to share the latest trends, challenges and opportunities facing companies, the tactics and tools needed to drive results, and a forum to make meaningful connections.

Our Alliance brand is renowned for its VIP dinners, exclusive breakfasts, and bespoke 1-2-1 meeting packages designed to bridge supply and demand across various verticals. Join our dynamic team and drive sales to new commercial prospects, creating valuable connections and driving business growth.



The Role

As a Digital & Advertising Sponsorship Executive, you’ll play a key role in connecting leading B2B brands with our highly engaged professional communities through impactful digital B2B demand generation, data, media services, online display advertising and influencer campaigns

From webinars and custom content, to virtual roundtables and lead generation programs, you’ll craft solutions that deliver genuine value and measurable ROI. This is a hands-on, commercially focused role for someone who thrives on building client relationships, solving problems creatively, and driving revenue.

You'll join a fast-paced, supportive team with strong growth potential. This is a hands-on commercial role perfect for someone eager to accelerate their career in digital media sales, advertising partnerships, and influencer marketing.



Key Responsibilities:

 - Drive Digital Growth: Own and grow your pipeline across digital sponsorship and advertising channels (including website placements, newsletter sponsorships, influencer programs & campaigns, and lead-gen content like webinars and white papers). Prospect, pitch, and close deals across both net-new and existing accounts.
   

 - Advertisement Focus: Package and sell advertising opportunities across our high-traffic websites, membership platforms, and digital communities, leveraging organic engagement and deep audience trust.
   

 - Revenue Performance: Consistently meet and exceed sales targets by maintaining strong pipeline discipline, accurate forecasting, and a results-driven mindset.
   

 - Advisor approach: Partner with clients to understand their goals and build solutions that drive measurable impact. Understand their business challenges, recommend tailored solutions, and feed insight back into our product and content roadmap.
   

 - Market Insight & Opportunity Creation: Stay ahead of trends within the SaaS, martech, AI, and digital education spaces. Identify new commercial opportunities and proactively propose ideas to capitalise on them.



Requirements
If the below sounds like you, we’d love to hear from you:

 - You have a deep understanding and proven track record of prospecting and developing strong relationships with enterprise customers (18 months + experience preferred)

 - You’ll be determined and focused, and enjoy the challenge of hunting and closing net new business

 - Tough targets won’t scare you and you’ll have a demonstrable track record of not just meeting them - but exceeding them

 - Confidence to experiment with different tactics, shake up strategy, adapt to new trends, to incorporate new products, changes, or to bounce back from subpar results

 - You want ownership in your role from the beginning and ready to level up.

 - You expertly balance and prioritise your daily to-do's, as well as your short-term and long-term planning

 - Experience with Asana or project management tools a plus (desirable)



Location: This role can be either Hybrid in our London office or completely remote across the UK (not abroad).

Salary: Base Salary range £32K - £38K (*Based on previous experience) - OTE £60K+

_______________________

source_jobs.title: Associate Account Manager (Sponsorship)
source_jobs.description:
The Company:

When it comes to digital communities, The Alliance https://allianceled.io/ is leading the way. Product marketing, sales enablement, product-led growth, AI, customer success, CMOs, you name it, we’re home to thriving communities for them all.

With 70,000+ professionals across our communities and a constant stream of events, memberships, and learning products, we help individuals and teams grow faster in their careers.

We’re ambitious, we move quickly, and we’re still early in the journey — which means real ownership, fast progression, and the opportunity to shape how our platform evolves.




THE ROLE

This is a client-facing, fast-paced role at the heart of our sponsorship business. As an Associate Account Manager, you’ll work closely with our sponsorship sales team to deliver standout experiences for our partners, across global events, VIP dinners, 1-2-1 meetings, and digital sponsorship campaigns.

You’ll be the bridge between what we sell and what we deliver. That means owning sponsor relationships, ensuring flawless execution, and helping clients see real value from their investment.

This role isn’t just about delivery, it’s about energy, presence, and ownership. You’ll be on-site at events, building relationships face-to-face, making things happen in real time, and representing The Alliance with confidence.


WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Client success & delivery: Ensure sponsors get what they signed up for across events, dinners, and digital campaigns, and that it lands well
   

 - Event execution (global): Be on-site at events, managing sponsors, handling logistics, and making sure everything runs smoothly
   

 - Sponsor relationships: Build strong, trusted relationships with clients, from onboarding through to delivery and beyond
   

 - Sales collaboration: Work closely with the sponsorship sales team to align on client goals, expectations, and opportunities
   

 - Performance & reporting: Track results, share performance insights, and help demonstrate ROI to clients
   

 - Account growth: Spot upsell and expansion opportunities and bring them back to the sales team
   

 - Operational excellence: Stay organised across multiple accounts, timelines, and deliverables, using tools and automation to stay efficient
   
   


WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE

You’ll be measured on a mix of delivery, client satisfaction, and growth:

 - Strong execution across events, digital campaigns, and client deliverables

 - High client satisfaction and clear demonstration of value

 - Consistent client check-ins and relationship building (QBR-style conversations)

 - Identifying and contributing to account expansion opportunities

 - Smooth onboarding and clear alignment on client goals from day one




PREVIOUS EXPERIENCE

 - You have some experience in B2B account management, client success, or events

 - You’re confident, personable, and love being in front of clients (especially in-person)

 - You bring energy, you’re someone people enjoy working with and remember after meeting

 - You’re highly organised and can juggle multiple projects without dropping the ball

 - You’re proactive, you don’t wait to be told what to do

 - You’re excited by travel and being on-site at global events



Compensation: £30,000-£35,000 Base salary *based on experience

Location: Please note, due to the travel component, we're looking for someone based in/around London.

Travel: This role may require travel to the US (and other countries). Check if your Country is covered by ESTA - Visa Waiver Program (VWP) here https://uk.usembassy.gov/visas/visa-waiver-program/non-immigrant-visa-waiver-program-vwp/#:~:text=Citizens%20of%20the%20United%20Kingdom,%2C%20Norway%2C%20Poland%2C%20Portugal%2C.

__________________________

Core Benefits:

 - Enhanced paid holiday: 34 days including UK Bank holidays and a day off on your birthday

 - Private Medical & Dental

 - Life Insurance: (4 x salary)

 - Early bird or night owl? Our flexible hours policy allows you

source_jobs.title: Head of Events Operations (12 Months FTC)
source_jobs.description:
When it comes to digital communities, The Alliance https://allianceled.io/ is leading the way. Product marketing, sales enablement, product-led growth, AI, customer success, CMOs, you name it, we’re home to thriving communities for them all. 

With 70,000+ professionals part of our communities and a never-ending supply of content, courses, events, and memberships, we are a key part of all our member’s growth. Adding value, authority, and leadership through our events is a key driver of our incredible growth. We engage attendees, speakers, partners, and members from the world's largest companies and most exciting start-ups. Think TikTok, Microsoft, Peloton, & Uber.





THE ROLE

We are seeking an experienced Head of Events Operations to join us on a 12-month maternity cover, taking ownership of the operational delivery of our global events portfolio.

Reporting directly to the COO, you will play a key leadership role across the business, overseeing the successful execution of in-person summits, exhibitions, awards, executive dinners, member events and bespoke training programmes.

This role combines strategic planning with hands-on delivery. You will lead a small team, manage key supplier relationships, drive operational excellence, and continuously improve how we plan and execute events at scale.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Own Event Delivery: Lead the end-to-end operational delivery of The Alliance's global events portfolio, ensuring events are delivered on time, within budget and aligned with business objectives.

 - Lead the Team: Manage, coach and develop the Events Operations team, setting priorities, allocating resources and ensuring consistent delivery across multiple concurrent projects.

 - Operational Excellence: Oversee every aspect of event operations including venue sourcing, logistics, supplier management, budgeting, registration, event technology, compliance and onsite execution.

 - Supplier Management: Build and maintain strong relationships with venues, suppliers and partners, negotiating contracts, managing performance and delivering value while maintaining exceptional quality.

 - Cross-functional Collaboration: Partner closely with Commercial, Production, Marketing and Membership teams to maximise event success, support revenue opportunities and deliver outstanding attendee experiences.

 - Continuous Improvement: Identify opportunities to improve processes, systems and ways of working, creating scalable operational frameworks that support future growth.

 - Performance & Insights: Monitor event performance, gather attendee feedback and implement improvements that strengthen operational efficiency and customer satisfaction.

 - Risk & Compliance: Ensure all events are delivered safely and compliantly, maintaining robust health & safety, risk management and operational procedures before, during and after every event.




REQUIREMENTS

 - Proven expertise in events delivery - proven, measurable success in the management of high-impact events from international large conferences, exhibitions, summits or comparable B2B events.

 - Previous experience managing and developing event teams.

 - Strong commercial awareness with experience managing budgets and supplier negotiations.

 - Proven ability to lead multiple complex projects simultaneously.

 - Excellent stakeholder management and communication skills.

 - Demonstrated experience improving processes, systems and operational efficiency.

 - Calm and confident decision-maker with strong problem-solving abilities.

 - Experience using project management tools such as Asana or equivalent.

 - Willingness to travel internationally and support occasional evening and weekend events

Being a startup, things here move quickly. You’ll be thrown last-minute curveballs and will need to be adaptable. We enjoy it. We need you to, too

 




CONTRACT DETAILS

Salary: £48,000–£50,000 FTE per annum, depending on experience.

Contract Length: 12 Months maternity cover (st

notes: No tech roles posted; sales and events operations dominate
